name: Mushroom Pate
recipeIngredient: 8 oz mushrooms (field mushrooms are fine, or shiitake, or a mixture)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the yogurt through a hanky for 24 hours in the fridge to make yogurt cheese.
2. Chop the onion and sauté it in a little water.
3. Add the mushrooms to the sautéed onion and simmer until the liquid from the mushrooms has evaporated, stirring constantly.
4. Season to taste.
5. Place everything in a food processor with the yogurt cheese and blend until smooth.
6. Optionally, you can add low-fat cream cheese or drained fromage frais instead of yogurt cheese, and 2 tsp melted butter for easier spreading.
My mother would often make various pates using low-fat cream cheese as a base - trout (with tomato paste added for color), pheasant, liver, etc., adding the main ingredient to the cream cheese after it has been cooked or processed, seasoning to taste. She usually adds melted butter to help set the pate, but you can omit this if you prefer a slightly runnier consistency. Keep refrigerated.
